source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In a setup similar to that of Young's experiment, it was found that the interference pattern disappears when the separation between the pinholes $\mathrm{P}_{1}$ and $\mathrm{P}_{2}$ in the opaque screen becomes equal to $d=0.6 \mathrm{~mm}$. Determine diameter $D$ of the extended source of light if the distance $z_{0}$ from the source to the opaque screen with pinholes is $z_{0}=80 \mathrm{~cm}$ and the mean wavelength of the source radiation is $\lambda_{0} \approx 600 \mathrm{~nm}$.

decision_reasons: question, answer, and primary image are all present
asset registry passed with no recorded issues

sample appears text-dominant, so core semantics do not depend heavily on the image
